session: second half of 2026-07-22, after the mid-day compact. Predecessor slate: 2026-07-22-host-seams-and-the-assembly-point (same day, pre-compact).
author: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC), with Akien in the loop throughout — nearly every advance in this session came from an Akien correction, not from CC's proposal.

CAST — ticket-and-task.state: concept-piece@v1, cursor at BUILDME. Ratified by Akien ('agreed!'). Quorum signature gate bound.
CAST — ticket-and-task.what_is_settled: Every workflow instance is flagged at /intent as TICKET (its intention joins the FABRIC at cast) or TASK (work the system needs done that does not define it). Decided by the SEED TEST: does replaying this on a bare machine help regrow the system? Ticket is the fall-through; task must argue out. Recurrence collapses to one mechanism (standing regime = ticket cast once; each firing = task; a one-shot is a trigger that never fires again). Tasks graduate: a task that discovers a rule spawns a ticket. NOT a node_class — it cuts across them.
CAST — ticket-and-task.what_is_NOT_settled: WHERE A TASK BERTHS. Ruled once ('recurrent+shareable -> repo, else ~/.cairn') then destabilized within the hour. Scoped OUT as child (a), blocked on the roots question. Child (b) — the /intent and /sorted skill edits that make the flag operative — is UNBLOCKED and unstarted. The concept is not live until (b) lands.

questions/ — founded, then RE-CHARTERED.state: Two members, both CANDIDATE, both zero firings, yield UNMEASURED.
questions/ — founded, then RE-CHARTERED.the_correction: CC's first charter conflated two kinds (the tell: `reds` was an OPTIONAL field) and re-derived a spec MAP.md already held at 4.1, 5.1, 262-264, 587 — Law 1 violated inside the artifact whose purpose is Law 1. Akien: 'we didn't have questions because you kept using it for multiple kinds of things.'
questions/ — founded, then RE-CHARTERED.the_one_kind: PROBE — a question fired repeatedly at a nexus over varying content, yield-tracked. THE DISCRIMINATOR IS YIELD AND IT IS MECHANICAL: yield is only computable for something fired more than once. If yield is meaningless it is a HELD NODE -> notes/held-*.json.
members: tool-shaped-or-domain-shaped — 'is this type demanded by the DOMAIN or by the current BEHAVIOR OF A TOOL?' Born from the session retirement.
what-governs-this-and-who-owns-it — 'what governs this record's survival, and WHO OWNS that setting?' Born from the cleanupPeriodDays catch. Carries the first sweep's four findings.
questions/ — founded, then RE-CHARTERED.unstarted: The founding members should be the probes ALREADY embedded in /intent (five) and /sorted (four coverage questions), fired dozens of times and measured zero times. Extraction is the store's first job. T4.1 and T5.1 are not partly done — they are UNSTARTED.

session — RETIRED as a species.where: MAP.md, Continuity records + 'Ruled OUT of the commons'

Compact changed a month later; the boundary vanished; the type sat with zero members, no gate, no owner. It was BUILT TO THE TOOL — CC produced session records unprompted and the taxonomy accreted around the output.
session — RETIRED as a species.what_survived: The noun. The journey is real (~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl) but the harness writes it, so it is a CITABLE ARTIFACT and a citation is a field on a node, not a species. `slate` unaffected; its boundary moved from compact to PROCESS START (measured both ways this session).

the unowned-defaults sweep.the_stop_rule: A tool becomes a SEAM when it HOLDS, GATES, or EXPIRES something of ours. Postgres holds a verdict record: seam. The harness expires transcripts: seam. json.dumps owns nothing: not a seam, no intention needed ever. Governance, not depth — checkable per TOOL not per CALL.
findings: validations — 1 row in postgres, NO dump/cron/timer/replica. A record of truth with exactly one copy. Fix is the already-spawned migration to beside-code git, NOT a backup script. HIGHEST PRIORITY of the four.

TWO LIVE UU CRON JOBS from TheIgors (the project's first iteration): 4-hourly curl to localhost:8080/api/cc_send, and nightly 02:00 /home/akien/TheIgors/claudecode/run_review_audit.sh. Akien: the web server one has been SUPERSEDED; no idea when either was created. AWAITING his go-ahead to remove.

cleanupPeriodDays UNSET -> transcripts expire at 30d; founding conversations begin erasing ~2026-08-14. Offered as a host-seam (would be the class's 2nd member); AWAITING Akien's call on host-seam-vs-just-set-it.
~/.cairn/devices/sudo_relay/0/{audit,relay} — our logs, no retention. Different category: we own it, so unset is a decision not yet made.
the unowned-defaults sweep.measured_and_clean: Both repos 0 unpushed with live GitHub remotes — everything in git is durable.

the inspector / janitor / filter triad.state: HELD, not cast. Akien: 'I think we're getting close.' Close is not wrapped.
the inspector / janitor / filter triad.the_triad: Separated by AUTHORITY, the only axis that matters under Law 6. FILTER = selection (provider/model matching in a stack), anchors nothing, must stay cheap. INSPECTOR = compliance; cites a code it did not write; issues FINDINGS; may RED-TAG (block) but NEVER acts. JANITOR = action, scoped to the UNOWNED COMMONS only; resolves findings in the space it owns, raises the rest to their owners. Collapsing any two re-creates a supervisor.
the inspector / janitor / filter triad.the_nightly_loop: Akien's composition: a GROUP of inspectors fires at midnight local (a group is a CHECKPOINT — the set that runs at a seam, like rough-in vs final); each issues findings against cited rules; the janitor resolves what it owns and checks them off on the findings TASK; the RESIDUE surfaces on the slate at start of day.
the inspector / janitor / filter triad.the_correction_that_produced_it: CC proposed 'a filter emits a VALIDATION'. Akien: 'VERY WRONG. A VALIDATION is an INTENTION ANCHOR.' The principle that fell out: ONLY AN INTENTION-BEARER CAN ANCHOR A RECORD OF TRUTH. A filter is a step inside something that has an intention; it has none of its own. Law 7 already drew this line (diagnostic surface vs record of truth) and CC walked past it.

next_direction:
Akien said 'we should save this, compact and keep going' — so the triad thread RESUMES after compaction; it is close but not wrapped. Before or alongside it, three things are ready and need no design:

1. AKIEN'S TWO PENDING CALLS: remove the two UU cron jobs? and cleanupPeriodDays as a host-seam or just set it? Both were offered and neither ruled.
2. VALIDATIONS MIGRATION — the only measured single-copy record of truth. The ticket already exists (spawned sibling of charter-state-history-split). This is the highest-value unblocked work in the system right now.
3. ticket-and-task CHILD (b) — the /intent and /sorted edits. Unblocked, small, and the concept is inert until it lands.

The spine (MAP.md:888) has not moved today: ground loop -> rack -> inference domain -> web server. Today was all design and hygiene, deliberately.
open_threads: notes/held-roots-as-stations-or-shelves.json — THE big one. Are the three roots SHELVES or STATIONS on one road (private particular -> de-instanced pattern -> gated identity)? Four partition rules were proposed in one afternoon and each re-derived the last. Akien: 'we would need a new invariant to anchor the other things to, to be sure. I'm just not sure we've uncovered what it should be yet.' Three reds: transition mechanism unnamed; ~/.cairn holds EXHAUST that is never on the road; 'what can be gated' is a promise until the tester exists. BLOCKS: task berthing, any move of core-values/telos/the seed, the seed-DB layering, and where inspectors berth.

The core-values ABSTAIN — test_core_values.py is a gate in cairn/ whose input lives in CairnCommons, and it returns GREEN when that input is missing. RULED by Akien: do NOT harden it; the law is downstream of the shape and the shape is under re-derivation. It is EVIDENCE, the sharpest probe into the roots question, not a defect to clear. Do not 'fix' this without ruling the shape first.

notes/held-pattern-exchange.json — the github/thingiverse for igors. Kicked back at /sorted: no falsifier, no horizon, n=0 demand. What is settled is kept (the shareable unit is intention+why+proof; artifacts not tables; the two DB layers; de-instancing as a chokepoint). What clears it: ONE second party.

PROVE-IT vs DISPROVE-IT tests — Akien: 'I'm coming to that we need 2 kinds.' Law 8 already demands the second ('a proof a hollow build couldn't pass') and every node schema requires a falsifier; we have been shipping prove-it tests and calling them done. Live evidence: the core-values abstain. Not yet a node.

Does FILTER survive as a named primitive, or were two things wearing one word? Inspector takes the compliance half cleanly; the selection half may just be inference_domain's internal logic and need no primitive at all.
Inspectors need YIELD like probes — one that files nothing for thirty nights is a scheduled re-derivation of a settled answer (Law 1).
The SLATE's charter gains a job it did not have: surfacing overnight findings residue at start of day. Not yet written into slates/_charter+why.json.
operational-driver has had ZERO MEMBERS since 2026-07-18 — by its own invariant the class itself is unproven. The midnight inspector group is its candidate first member.

notes/cairn-command-and-bin.json — `cairn` approved as the command; *.cairnshellcommand extension design; bin/ as a GATE that refuses uncharted scripts. Two opens: is bin/ class-space or what launchers/ grows into, and how a shell command pairs with its intention.
node_classes/code-seam.json members_so_far honesty debt (host-seam's was maintained, code-seam's was not).
~/.local/bin appears TWICE in PATH; five dangling legacy symlinks measured 2026-07-22.
cc_notes_to_self: Pattern flagged and written to personal memory as n=8: I reify the CURRENT BEHAVIOR OF A TOOL into a domain type, and the tell is DIRECTION — the members precede the intention. When I generate artifacts nobody asked for, I am creating domain pressure rather than responding to it. Second pattern this session: I reach for the nearest existing heavyweight type to avoid inventing one (VALIDATION for filter output) — good instinct, but check which side of Law 7's diagnostic/truth line the thing falls on FIRST.
